abstract:
We present BEAST 2, a new open-source software platform for Bayesian evolutionary analysis designed for enhanced flexibility and extensibility. This platform represents a fundamental re-engineering of the original BEAST 1 framework, addressing structural limitations that emerged as the software matured. A primary focus of this redesign is post-deployment extensibility; BEAST 2 introduces a robust package management system that enables third-party developers to integrate new functionality and models directly into the platform without requiring a centralized software release. We demonstrate the power of this architecture through several recently published packages, including birth-death-sampling tree priors, phylodynamic tools, and model-averaging techniques for substitution models and site partitioning. 

In addition to its modularity, BEAST 2 improves computational resilience and efficiency by allowing the entire state of the MCMC chain to be read from or written to disk. This functionality facilitates seamless checkpointing and provides better support for multi-processor and high-performance computing environments. Finally, the integration between the analysis engine and the user interface (BEAUti 2) has been streamlined. Through a simple XML template mechanism, new packages can now automatically extend the graphical interface, ensuring that BEAST and BEAUti operate on a unified file format. BEAST 2 is cross-platform, licensed under the GNU LGPL, and available at http://beast2.org.
